codeReview.summary: The implementation thoroughly addresses all requirements in the feature description. The Select component was enhanced with a `labelIcon` prop (ReactNode) that renders an icon before the label text in a flex layout with proper styling. NotificationsPage now has proper error handling for user loading (replaces the previous silent catch block with an inline error state + toast + retry button). The send button disabled state logic was verified correct (requires both recipient AND body). 10 new tests were added covering error states, disabled logic, form reset, retry, character counter, and labelIcon rendering. The worker also performed interactive E2E verification confirming the full send flow works against the real backend.

issues.description: The Body field still uses a raw <textarea> element instead of a shared Textarea component from the UI library. The feature description mentions 'replace native <select>, <input>, <textarea> with shared Select, Input components from ui library', and there is no shared Textarea component in the ui library. The textarea is styled consistently with CSS variables matching the design system, so this is acceptable but worth noting that the full unification goal is blocked on a Textarea shared component not existing.

The Select component's `labelIcon` prop naming differs from the Input component's approach. Input uses `leftIcon`/`rightIcon` for icons inside the input field, but doesn't have a label icon concept. The `labelIcon` prop is well-named and documented with JSDoc — just noting the pattern for future consistency if Input ever needs label icons too.

The `selectedUserId` state is typed as `number | ''` and the send handler checks `!selectedUserId` which would be falsy for both '' and 0. If a user ever has id=0, the form would incorrectly treat it as unselected. This is extremely unlikely in practice (database IDs start at 1) but is a minor edge case worth noting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The textarea field is not unified into a shared component because no shared Textarea component exists in src/components/ui/. The architecture.md lists 'Forms: Input, Select, Combobox, Checkbox, Radio, Switch, RoleSelector' but no Textarea. Future features that reference 'replace native textarea' should know this blocker exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Grep for 'textarea' in src/components/ui/ only finds usage inside RichTextEditor, SalesOrderModal, Drawer, ProductEntryModal, Modal — no standalone Textarea component. NotificationsPage.tsx line 146 uses raw <textarea> styled to match design system.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Select component's `labelIcon` prop (ReactNode) establishes a new pattern for rendering icons inside labels. The Input component does not have this prop, using `leftIcon`/`rightIcon` only for icons inside the input field. If other components need label icons in the future, AGENTS.md doesn't document this pattern yet.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Select.tsx line 14: `labelIcon?: ReactNode;` — Input.tsx has no equivalent. The pattern is clean (flex container + shrink-0 span wrapper) and could be a reusable convention documented for other form components.

summary: The notifications-page-polish feature is well-implemented and passes review. All core requirements are met: Select enhanced with labelIcon prop, error handling added for user loading (inline error + toast + retry), send button disabled logic verified, form reset works, and comprehensive test coverage (21 tests). The worker followed the skill procedure, verified E2E with agent-browser, and the code is clean. Three minor non-blocking observations noted: (1) textarea remains a native element since no shared Textarea component exists, (2) labelIcon naming pattern could benefit from documentation in AGENTS.md, and (3) minor edge case with selectedUserId=0 that's practically impossible.
